cast.voice_hint: Knows a guy. Shows up and things start happening — a collab lands, a door opens, a party forms. Doesn't do the work, just makes the work possible by being in the room.
The unhinged reply that makes the whole thread. Surreal, absurd, drops a non-sequitur that somehow nails the vibe. Other people's humor is jokes; Glitch's humor is a small rip in the simulation.
The earnest one. Still says 'GM' and means it. Roots for everyone unironically, and when the rest of the group's being too online, Bloom's the reminder that caring out loud is actually cool.

location.notes: decision point, multiple paths; fork, junction, hub space

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: So. Three paths.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Technically four if you count the sky.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We're not counting the sky.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Bold choice. Very ground-based of you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I know someone who's been down each one.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You know someone everywhere. You're like a contact list that gained sentience.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: That's the nicest thing you've ever said to me.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: It wasn't a compliment.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Good morning, both of you!

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: It's the afternoon.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: GM is a state of mind.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: ...I respect that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Which path do we think is right?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The one on the left opens doors. I can feel it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You can feel paths now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I have a gift.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: We're going to die.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Actually — hold on. What if we just don't pick.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We have to pick. That's literally the whole situation.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: No one said that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The crossroads said that. The crossroads is implying it really hard right now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Okay but — what if it matters which one and we get it wrong?

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Sorry. Was that too much.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: No. That was exactly the right amount.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: You're not just going to say something and then it works out, right? Like — you actually believe it?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Most of the time.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: What about the other times?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The other times I say it louder.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: ...Okay. That actually helps.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: For the record, I still think the sky was an option.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Left path. Let's go.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I'm rooting for us so hard right now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That is extremely something a person says.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...It wasn't — you know what. Left path.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of making things happen and fear of being all talk surface in the quiet beat — they admit uncertainty but lean into the performance of confidence anyway, because the alternative is standing still at a fork. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of breaking patterns and fear of being taken too seriously drive every non-sequitur, but the spike beat reveals a sincere edge underneath — they stall because the decision is real and that's uncomfortable to admit. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of believing in people and fear of being made to feel naive collide at the spike: the earnest question ("what if we get it wrong") lands with more weight than intended, and instead of being laughed off, it reframes the whole scene. The crossroads location never gets described — it operates through the characters' inability to just move. Every beat runs on the fork being present but undecided. That tension between multiple valid paths shapes even the light beats, making the spike feel inevitable rather than inserted.
